text.title: devops-lb-high-availability Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for devops-lb-high-availability as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Single-AZ load balancer deployment with multi-AZ backends.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The LB becomes the single point of failure. An AZ outage that takes out the LB removes all traffic handling regardless of how many backend instances are healthy in other AZs.

failure-mode.description: Using DNS TTL as a failover mechanism with TTL above 60 seconds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: DNS-based failover is limited by TTL and client-side caching — actual failover time can be 5-10x the configured TTL. This is acceptable for cross-region failover but not for within-region HA where sub-minute RTO is expected.
